name: Canada
summary: English-speaking with a best-in-class 3-year citizenship and 3-year master's PGWP, but 2025-26 study caps, ~36% national study-permit approval, dormant STEM Express Entry draws and tightened spousal work permits create real friction for a two-engineer Bangladeshi couple.

links.title: IRCC immigrate to Canada (official)

job-market.summary: Deep English-speaking demand for software/AI talent, but 2025-26 is selective hiring amid major layoffs and newcomer friction.
job-market.reasoning: Toronto, Vancouver, Montreal and Waterloo host large tech sectors and Robert Half's 2026 guide keeps software/AI roles in the most-in-demand list with specialized talent scarce; however 2026 saw heavy layoffs (Microsoft ~6,000, Meta ~8,000 globally, Ontario ~7,348 cuts), so two newcomers without Canadian experience face real friction before landing roles. Both engineers are hireable but placement is not immediate.
evidence: Robert Half 2026: software engineers among top in-demand tech roles; specialized AI talent scarce
~9,218 software engineer postings open in Canada (Glassdoor, May 2026)
2026 layoffs: Ontario ~7,348 and BC ~2,595 cuts; market shifted to selective hiring

links.title: Government of Canada Job Bank
Robert Half - most in-demand tech jobs in Canada 2026

visa-access.summary: Biometrics in Dhaka, but no SDS, ~36% national approval, CAD 22,895 funds bar; medicals assessed via New Delhi RMO.
visa-access.reasoning: Bangladeshis apply via the regular stream (SDS ended 8 Nov 2024) with biometrics at VFS Dhaka and IOM medicals in Dhaka, but the 2025 national study-permit approval rate fell to about 35.7% and 'financial insufficiency' is a top refusal reason. Proof-of-funds rose to CAD 22,895 per applicant (1 Sep 2025) on top of tuition, and medical results route to IRCC's New Delhi Regional Medical Office, so processing is local for biometrics but the funds/scrutiny bar is heavy.
evidence: SDS closed 8 Nov 2024 - all Bangladeshi applicants use the regular stream
2025 national study-permit approval ~35.7% (refusals ~62%); financial insufficiency a top Dhaka refusal reason
Proof-of-funds CAD 22,895 + tuition per applicant (from 1 Sep 2025); biometrics in Dhaka, medicals via New Delhi RMO

links.title: IRCC study permit (official)
Increased fund requirements for study permits (CAD 22,895)

citizenship.summary: Citizenship after 1,095 days (3 of 5 yrs); only CLB 4 language; dual citizenship fully allowed - top of the set.
citizenship.reasoning: Among the fastest routes globally for this couple: physically present 1,095 days in the 5 years before applying (min 730 as PR), a modest CLB 4 speaking/listening test for ages 18-54, and Canada is one of the most dual-citizenship-friendly nations so they need not renounce Bangladeshi nationality. IRCC quotes 7-14 months processing (routine files often 8-11), keeping the passport timeline realistic.
evidence: 1,095 days physical presence in 5 years (min 730 as PR)
Language only CLB 4 (speak/listen) for ages 18-54; IRCC processing ~7-14 months in 2026
Dual citizenship permitted - no need to give up the Bangladeshi passport

links.title: IRCC Canadian citizenship (official)
The 1095-day rule for Canadian citizenship

post-study-work.summary: Master's grads get a 3-year open PGWP, field-of-study exempt; CLB 7 language proof required (easy for IT).
post-study-work.reasoning: Graduates of a university master's program receive an open PGWP of up to 3 years regardless of program length (min 8 months) and are exempt from the field-of-study restriction, letting both engineers work openly and switch employers. Since 1 Nov 2024 degree grads must show CLB 7 across all four skills, which is straightforward for IT professionals; IRCC confirmed the eligible-fields list is frozen for 2026.
evidence: Master's grads: up to 3-year open PGWP regardless of program length
Degree grads exempt from PGWP field-of-study list (frozen for 2026)
CLB 7 in all four skills required for university grads (since 1 Nov 2024)

links.title: IRCC Post-Graduation Work Permit (official)
Eligible fields of study for PGWP will not change in 2026

spouse-family.summary: Both can accompany; spousal open work permit survives for a 16+ month master's but 2025-26 rules narrowed it.
spouse-family.reasoning: From 21 Jan 2025 a spousal open work permit (SOWP) is limited to spouses of master's programs of at least 16 months, doctoral or listed professional programs, so a standard thesis MSc qualifies; an additional rule effective 4 Mar 2026 refuses SOWPs when the student is in their final academic term. In the work phase the principal must hold a skilled job (TEER 0/1) with 16+ months authorization remaining, so both can work but only under conditions, not unconditionally as they would on a points-based skilled visa.
evidence: SOWP for students restricted to master's >=16 months / doctoral / professional programs (21 Jan 2025)
Effective 4 Mar 2026: SOWP refused if student is in their final academic term
Work-phase SOWP requires principal in a skilled occupation with 16+ months authorization remaining

links.title: IRCC open work permits for spouses (eligibility)
IRCC work permit for spouses of students

msc-study.summary: Abundant English MSc; high coursework tuition (~CAD 35k/yr) though research MSc often funded; 24h/wk work.
msc-study.reasoning: Broad English-taught master's catalogue at strong universities, with coursework tuition around CAD 35,000/yr, while thesis/research MSc commonly carry TA/RA stipends and awards (CGS-M, Vanier CAD 50k). Master's and doctoral students are exempt from the study-permit cap from 1 Jan 2026, students may work off-campus up to 24 hours/week, and the spouse can accompany - though sticker tuition and the funds bar keep this short of best-in-class.
evidence: Wide English-taught MSc catalogue; coursework tuition ~CAD 35,000/yr
Research MSc often funded via TA/RA + CGS-M / Vanier (CAD 50k) awards
Master's/doctoral students exempt from the study-permit cap from 1 Jan 2026; off-campus work 24 hrs/week

links.title: IRCC study in Canada (official)
What international students need to know about the 2026-2028 plan

pr-pathway.summary: Clear ~3.5-4yr study->PGWP->CEC route, but CEC CRS ~507-515 and STEM draws dormant ~23 months.
pr-pathway.reasoning: Express Entry (CEC after 1 yr skilled Canadian work) is a predictable route taking roughly 3.5-4 years from study start with PR processing of ~6 months, but 2026 CEC cut-offs sit at a high 507-515 and dedicated STEM category draws have not run for about 23 months as of mid-2026. French at NCLC 7 unlocks far lower category cut-offs, so PR is attainable but competitive without French or a provincial nomination.
evidence: Study->PGWP->CEC pathway ~3.5-4 years; economic PR target 380k in 2026 (64% economic share)
2026 CEC CRS cut-offs ~507-515; STEM category draws dormant ~23 months as of June 2026
French-language category draws cut much lower; OINP / BC PNP Tech are active alternatives

links.title: IRCC Express Entry (official)
CEC cut-off reaches new high - April 2026 draw (CIC News)

income-cost.summary: Strong dual tech salaries (SWE ~CAD 86-162k) and softening rents, but big-metro housing still erodes savings.
income-cost.reasoning: Robert Half's 2026 guide puts software engineers at CAD 86,250-131,500 and seniors at 119,750-162,500, so a two-engineer household earns well. Toronto 1-bed rents eased to roughly CAD 2,100-2,340/month (down year-over-year) and Vancouver ~CAD 2,260, so dual income yields good savings, but high-metro housing and tax keep it short of exceptional.
evidence: Robert Half 2026: software engineer CAD 86,250-131,500; senior 119,750-162,500
Toronto 1-bed rent ~CAD 2,100-2,340/month (down YoY); Vancouver ~CAD 2,262
Dual income materially improves household savings vs a single earner

links.title: Robert Half 2026 IT & tech salary guide (Canada)
Statistics Canada

healthcare.summary: Universal and free for PR/citizens, high quality, but record ~28.6-week waits and family-doctor shortage.
healthcare.reasoning: Provincial plans cover permanent residents and citizens (waiting period capped at 3 months, often waived) at no point-of-care cost and quality is high. However the Fraser Institute's 2025 study put the median wait from GP referral to treatment at 28.6 weeks (second-longest on record), millions lack a family doctor, and international students must buy provincial/private student insurance during study.
evidence: Universal provincial coverage for PR/citizens; <=3-month wait period, often waived
Median treatment wait 28.6 weeks in 2025 (Fraser Institute); Ontario shortest at 19.2 weeks
Family-doctor shortage; international students need student health insurance

links.title: Government of Canada - access universal health care
Fraser Institute - Waiting Your Turn 2025

culture-language.summary: English daily and official multiculturalism; no language barrier for PR or citizenship outside Quebec.
culture-language.reasoning: English (and French in Quebec) is the working language, Canada has an official multiculturalism policy and a very large immigrant population, and PR/citizenship language thresholds (CLB 7 / CLB 4) are easily met by IT professionals - so day-to-day integration is straightforward. Only Quebec adds a French dimension, which this couple can avoid by settling in Ontario/BC.
evidence: English working language outside Quebec; official multiculturalism policy
Large immigrant share of population eases integration
No additional language barrier for English-speaking PR/citizenship applicants

links.title: Government of Canada multiculturalism
IRCC citizenship language proof (CLB requirement)

safety-law.summary: Top-tier rule of law (WJP 13th, 0.79) and peaceful (GPI 14th); strong Charter protections for immigrants.
safety-law.reasoning: Canada ranks 13th of 143 on the 2025 WJP Rule of Law Index (score 0.79) and 14th of 163 on the 2025 Global Peace Index (1.491), with Charter rights and broadly safe cities. The GPI score edged down from 1.449 in 2024 and violent crime has risen over the decade, slightly tempering an otherwise strong score.
evidence: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025: 13th of 143 (score 0.79)
Global Peace Index 2025: 14th of 163 (1.491, down from 1.449 in 2024)
Charter rights and strong protections for immigrants

links.title: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025 - global press release
WJP Rule of Law Index - Canada insights

politics.summary: Stable democracy but sharp 2025-28 immigration cuts and rising public sentiment add volatility.
politics.reasoning: Institutions are stable, but the 2026-2028 plan holds PR admissions at 380,000, cut new study-permit intake roughly 50% to 155,000, and reduced total new temporary residents to 385,000 amid waning public support for immigration. Master's/doctoral students are exempted from the study cap from 1 Jan 2026, which softens the impact for this couple, but the overall policy direction is clearly tightening.
evidence: 2026-2028 plan: PR target 380k in 2026; economic share rising to ~64%
New study permits cut to 155k (~50% reduction); new temporary residents to 385k (2026)
Master's/doctoral students exempt from the study-permit cap (1 Jan 2026)

links.title: IRCC 2026-2028 Immigration Levels Plan (official)
What international students need to know about the 2026-2028 plan

tax.summary: Moderate burden: ~43% marginal at CAD 100k in Ontario; OECD single-worker tax wedge 32.0% (26th lowest).
tax.reasoning: The combined federal-provincial marginal rate is about 43% at CAD 100k in Ontario (top combined ~53.5%), but the OECD single-worker tax wedge is 32.0% - the 26th lowest of 38 OECD members - so the effective burden on tech salaries is moderate rather than punishing. The lowest federal personal rate is 14% for 2026, modestly improving take-home.
evidence: Ontario combined marginal rate ~43% at CAD 100k; top combined ~53.5%
OECD single-worker tax wedge 32.0% (2024) - 26th lowest of 38 OECD members
Lowest federal PIT rate 14% for 2026 (down from 15%)

links.title: OECD Taxing Wages 2026 - Canada
TaxTips.ca - combined federal & Ontario tax rates

muslim-diaspora.summary: Large Muslim (~1.8M) and Bangladeshi (~75-120k) communities and ubiquitous halal, but Islamophobia rising since 2023.
muslim-diaspora.reasoning: Canada has ~1.78M Muslims (4.9%, 2021 census) concentrated in the GTA, and roughly 75,425 single-origin to ~120,000 diaspora Bangladeshi Canadians, with about 26,650 in the City of Toronto - so halal food, mosques and a familiar South-Asian community (19% of the GTA) are readily available. The trend is the caveat: anti-Muslim and anti-Palestinian incidents have surged since October 2023, with about half of Canadians saying the country has a serious anti-Muslim problem.
evidence: ~1.78M Muslims in Canada (4.9%, 2021 census); South Asians ~19% of the GTA
~75,425 single-origin Bangladeshi Canadians (~120k diaspora estimate); ~26,650 in the City of Toronto (2021)
Islamophobia surge since Oct 2023 noted by advocacy groups; rising hostility is the main risk

links.title: Statistics Canada - religion and ethnocultural data
Combatting Islamophobia in Canada - 2024-2025 annual report
